midye dolma
Turkish stuffed mussels; "midye" (mussel) is unrecognizable to non-Turkish speakers
moules frites
French-Belgian classic; "moules" is French for mussels, unfamiliar to many English-speaking diners
takoyaki
"Tako" is Japanese for octopus, a mollusc/cephalopod; not obvious to non-Japanese-speaking diners
arroz negro
Spanish "black rice" gets its color and flavor from squid ink
cioppino
Italian-American seafood stew is almost always loaded with clams, mussels, and squid, none named in the dish title
jjamppong
Korean spicy seafood noodle soup routinely contains mussels, squid, and clams; not conveyed by the Korean name
conpoy
Cantonese dried scallop used whole or shredded as a savory garnish/flavoring in soups and rice dishes
haemul pajeon
Korean seafood pancake; "haemul" means seafood in Korean and typically includes squid and mussels
pad see ew
Thai stir-fried noodle dish is almost always seasoned with oyster sauce
xo sauce
Hong Kong luxury condiment made from dried scallops, a mollusc; appears in stir-fries without disclosure
char kway teow
Malaysian/Singaporean stir-fried noodles traditionally include cockles, a mollusc
fideua
Spanish noodle-paella dish usually includes mixed seafood such as squid and mussels
bouillabaisse
French seafood stew traditionally includes mussels and sometimes small squid
chow fun
Cantonese wide rice-noodle stir-fry (e.g. beef chow fun) is typically finished with oyster sauce
fritto misto
Italian mixed fry typically includes squid rings among the fried items
chow mein
Chinese-American stir-fried noodles are frequently sauced with oyster sauce
pad kra pao
Thai holy basil stir-fry is commonly seasoned with oyster sauce
buddha's delight
non-strict-vegetarian restaurant versions of this Chinese vegetable dish are sometimes cooked with oyster sauce despite the vegetarian-sounding name
chop suey
Chinese-American stir-fry is sometimes finished with oyster sauce
